review_contract.target_scope: context:guard PreToolUse hook and context-search intelligence surface
forbidden_targets: destructive writes outside controlled local fixtures

tools.name: semgrep
tools.purpose: SAST baseline unavailable in local PATH

tools.name: trivy
tools.purpose: SCA baseline unavailable in local PATH

tools.name: gitleaks
tools.purpose: secret scanner unavailable in local PATH; fallback used ripgrep secret-pattern scan

threat_surfaces.scope: context files, rule content, guard additionalContext payloads, and stale handoff state
evidence: src/context-guard.js reads only salient rule files selected by hard context:brief signals.
tests/context-guard.test.js covers unrelated-rule suppression, path-scoped guard suppression, and JSON wire payload stripping.

threat_surfaces.scope: Claude PreToolUse hook command, event-file handling, stdin JSON parsing, and shell command construction
evidence: src/commands/hooks-install.js interpolates agentName into shell command strings without validation or quoting.
Dry-run PoC generated command: aioson context:guard "$PWD" --tool=claude --agent=dev; echo AIOSON_PENTEST --json 2>/dev/null || true

threat_surfaces.scope: last-handoff and workflow handoff state affected by the review cycle
evidence: Workflow handoff state was produced by workflow:next and not by the hook payload.
No attacker-controlled handoff field is consumed by context:guard in the reviewed path.

threat_surfaces.scope: context:guard command contract and hooks-install generated settings
evidence: context:guard returns pristine hook JSON in --json mode and strips internal _guard observability data.
tests/hooks-install-guard.test.js covers hook presence, opt-out, and AIOSON-owned hook detection.
Focused ripgrep secret-pattern scan over reviewed source, tests, context report, pulse, scope-check, and rules found no real credentials.
context:guard derives search query from file path/content but injects only extracted rule constraints, not edited file content.

threat_surfaces.scope: advisory hook semantics, non-blocking behavior, and agent option propagation
evidence: The guard is intentionally advisory and always uses best-effort shell suffixes.
The same unvalidated agentName issue is tracked as SF-context-intelligence-01 because it becomes a shell command-construction vulnerability.

threat_surfaces.scope: agent identity passed into context:guard and hook configuration
evidence: src/commands/hooks-install.js accepts options.agent as a raw string after stripping only a leading @.
The untrusted identity string is embedded into Claude, Antigravity, and Codex hook command templates.

threat_surfaces.scope: hooks-install writes to Claude settings and preserves user hooks
evidence: hooks:install --dry-run --json shows the malicious agent string persisted into generated Claude hook commands.
installClaudeHooks writes the merged hook configuration to the global Claude settings file when not in dry-run mode.

threat_surfaces.skip_reason: The reviewed patch does not touch package manifests, lockfiles, GitHub Actions, release, publish, or third-party code-fetch paths.

findings.title: hooks:install interpolates --agent into persistent shell hooks without validation
findings.hypothesis: A crafted agent name can break out of the intended --agent argument and inject an additional shell command into generated AIOSON hook commands.
findings.attack_path: Run hooks:install with a crafted local --agent value; the generated Claude/Antigravity/Codex hook command persists the injected shell metacharacters and executes them when the hook fires.
preconditions: Attacker can influence the local hooks:install --agent argument, or convince the operator/automation to run hooks:install with a crafted agent name.
The generated hook is installed or inspected from dry-run output and then persisted by a non-dry-run install.
The target AI tool executes hook commands through a shell.
reproduction_steps: Run: node bin/aioson.js hooks:install . --tool=claude --agent="dev; echo AIOSON_PENTEST" --dry-run --json
Observe PostToolUse, Stop, and PreToolUse command strings containing '; echo AIOSON_PENTEST'.
Run: node -e "const { buildClaudeHooks } = require('./src/commands/hooks-install'); const hooks = buildClaudeHooks('dev; echo AIOSON_PENTEST'); console.log(hooks.PreToolUse[0].hooks[0].command);"
Observe the generated PreToolUse command: aioson context:guard "$PWD" --tool=claude --agent=dev; echo AIOSON_PENTEST --json 2>/dev/null || true
evidence: src/commands/hooks-install.js:35 builds `aioson hooks:emit "$PWD" --agent=${agentName} ...`.
src/commands/hooks-install.js:39 builds `aioson agent:done "$PWD" --agent=${agentName} --summary="Session ended via ${agentName} hook" ...`.
src/commands/hooks-install.js:47 builds `aioson context:guard "$PWD" --tool=claude --agent=${agentName} --json ...`.
src/commands/hooks-install.js:158 builds `aioson live:start "$PWD" --agent=${agentName} --tool=antigravity ...`.
src/commands/hooks-install.js:237 writes `AGENT="${agentName}"` into the Codex wrapper script.
src/commands/hooks-install.js:292 derives agentName from `options.agent` by only stripping a leading @.
tests/hooks-install-guard.test.js:28 currently asserts the requested agent is baked into the guard command, but does not assert validation or shell escaping.
Dry-run CLI output included `command`: `aioson context:guard "$PWD" --tool=claude --agent=dev; echo AIOSON_PENTEST --json 2>/dev/null || true`.
findings.impact: A malicious or compromised local project/workflow can turn a hook installation into persistent local command execution under the operator's account. Because hooks are written into global tool settings, the injected command may run later on ordinary Write/Edit/Stop events, outside the original installation context.
findings.suggested_fix: Normalize and validate agent names before command generation using the same canonical agent-name contract used elsewhere in the CLI, e.g. allow only known agents or a strict kebab-case identifier `/^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$/`. Reject invalid names in hooks:install and hooks:uninstall. Additionally shell-quote every dynamic argument used in generated command strings, and escape embedded values in the Codex wrapper script. Add regression tests for semicolon, quote, command-substitution, whitespace, and newline payloads across Claude, Antigravity, and Codex hook builders.
findings.fix_summary: hooks:install and hooks:uninstall now normalize agent names to known agent ids or strict kebab-case identifiers before hook generation, reject shell-control payloads, and shell-quote dynamic hook command arguments.
